expectations.evidence: invented account name(s) present / real codes missing

analyst_notes: Pass rate +53 pts (96% vs 43%). The lift is concentrated in GL-code fidelity and the recoverable-vs-borne control: the baseline invents placeholder codes ('Due from Owner', 1310/6410/2000) on every eval, and gets the recoverable VAT treatment wrong twice — eval-0 passes input VAT through to the owner, eval-1 expenses a recoverable DEWA bill instead of parking it on the landlord receivable.

Baseline variance is high (±29%) because eval difficulty varies: it scored 1/9 on the multi-bill batch (eval-0) but 4/6 on the single RCM invoice (eval-2), where general accounting knowledge alone gets RCM and the flag behaviour mostly right. The skill's value is largest on routine multi-bill close work, exactly the high-volume case.

Non-discriminating assertions (both configs pass): every-entry-balances, premise->unit mapping, RCM-nets-to-nil, and raising the unknown-unit flag. These are correctness floors, not skill differentiators — keep them as guards but read the GL-code/control assertions for skill lift.

The only with_skill miss is eval-1's DEWA bill being split into two sub-entries (consumption + municipality) with a verbatim-duplicated f8 review flag. Cosmetic but messy for a reviewer. Iteration-2 target: book one entry per invoice with combined components, and de-duplicate review flags.

Open design question surfaced by eval-2: when unit_type is unknown the skill defaults to rev-share/recoverable (landlord receivable) and flags it; the baseline defaulted to leased/Silkhaus-borne (conservative). Worth a controller decision on which default is safer for Silkhaus.

Cost of the skill: +12s (~87s vs 75s) and +14.5k tokens (~61.5k vs 47k) per task, from loading posting_rules.md and running build_je.py. For a finance close where a mis-coded or one-sided entry is expensive to unwind, this is a strongly favourable trade.

Caveat: 1 run per eval per config, so the reported ±stddev is across the 3 evals, not run-to-run noise. Treat absolute pass rates as directional; the +53 pt gap is large enough to be robust to that caveat.
